content.problemStatement:
Chef is a software developer, so he has to switch between different languages sometimes. Each programming language has some features, which are represented by integers here.

Currently, Chef has to use a language with two given features AAA and BBB. He has two options --- switching to a language with two features A1A_1A1​ and B1B_1B1​, or to a language with two features A2A_2A2​ and B2B_2B2​. All four features of these two languages are pairwise distinct.

Tell Chef whether he can use the first language, the second language or neither of these languages (if no single language has all the required features).


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains six space-separated integers A,B,A1,B1,A2,B2A, B, A_1, B_1, A_2, B_2A,B,A1​,B1​,A2​,B2​.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing the integer 111 if Chef should switch to the first language, or 222 if Chef should switch to the second language, or 000 if Chef cannot switch to either language.
constraints: All four features of the two candidate languages (A1, B1, A2, B2) are pairwise distinct.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains six space‑separated integers A B A1 B1 A2 B2.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line: 1 if the required features match the first language, 2 if they match the second language, otherwise 0.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: {1,2} equals {2,1} → first language → output 1.\nTest case 2: {3,4} equals {4,3} → second language → output 2.\nTest case 3: {1,2} matches neither language → output 0.

solution.approach: For each test case we only need to compare two unordered pairs. Create the unordered pair {A,B}. If it equals {A1,B1} output 1, else if it equals {A2,B2} output 2, otherwise output 0. This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
